goal: Split the outer b-block online-softmax carry into K independent block partitions, stage partial (m, den, pv) state in LDS, then combine once without increasing VGPR pressure.
why: ctx slope is dominated by the outer b-block carry; inner tt split was refuted and raised VGPR pressure.

lowering.what: splits the serial b REDUCE loop into K independent partitions (private state + private LDS tile) + flash log-sum-exp combine; default-off, cache-keyed, byte-identical when unset
lowering.refutation: Partition independence requires privatizing the online-softmax state + transient compute regs (VGPR 88->176) and the K/V tile (LDS 8192->16384). The occupancy tax outweighs the ILP/latency-overlap gain on this occupancy-bound, HBM-leaning tile -> ~18% slower at both ctx. Same failure class as the refuted SCHED_UNROLL_SPLIT (ILP-via-state); confirms diagnostic-truth-#2. LDS-staging the partials does not escape it: the hot online-softmax accumulators must be register-resident in the inner tt loop, and independence requires private registers either way.
lowering.next_layer: b-parallelism must come from MORE WORKGROUPS (more s-splits / smaller L), not within-warp split -- that lever is capped by the split-KV combine economics (already characterized). The within-tile lever is work-REMOVAL, not ILP-via-state.

kill_conditions: VGPR rises above 88 or wg/CU drops below 4.0
selected outer-loop ds_bpermute or waitcnt count does not improve
ctx4096 does not improve after correctness and route-clean gates
candidate falls back to owned/manual attention route
